channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Two private lunar landers head toward the moon in a roundabout journey
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — In a two-for-one moonshot, SpaceX launched a pair of lunar landers Wednesday for U.S. and Japanese companies looking to jumpstart business on Earth’s dusty sidekick.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The long struggle to establish Martin Luther King Jr. Day
item.description: The Rev. Martin Luther King Jr. gave his iconic “I Have a Dream” speech in 1963 on the steps of the Lincoln Memorial. He chose that location in part to honor President Abraham Lincoln as “a great American, in whose symbolic shadow we stand today.” Now, millions of people honor King in the same way.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Relief, 21st-century style: As wildfires burn, GoFundMe becomes a repository of harrowing stories
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — They seem endless, these sapping stories of loss. A grandfather starts over in his 90s. A family loses their dream home. People who were already struggling are dealt new, brutal blows.
item.dc:creator: MATT SEDENSKY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a potential 2028 candidate, wants to find common ground with Trump
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer wants a fresh start with President-elect Donald Trump but also warns that she won't back down over tariffs that she believes would hurt the auto industry in her state.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and ISABELLA VOLMERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden promised to turn the page on Trump. Now he's being replaced by him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden promised nothing short of a national exorcism when he took office. He wanted to “restore the soul" of the country and prove that Donald Trump was only a footnote in the American story, not its next chapter.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: As Los Angeles burns, Hollywood's Oscar season turns into a pledge drive
item.description: When the Palisades Fire broke out in Los Angeles last Tuesday, Hollywood’s awards season was in full swing. The Golden Globes had transpired less than 48 hours earlier and a series of splashy awards banquets followed in the days after.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: John Ratcliffe, tapped by Trump to lead the CIA, will face questioning in the Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lawmakers on Capitol Hill on Wednesday will question President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the CIA on his vision for America's premier spy agency.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 killed in Mexican town caught between battling criminal groups
item.description: HUITZILAC, Mexico (AP) — Schools and some businesses were closed and few people walked the streets of this town south of Mexico’s capital Tuesday, hours after five people were gunned down on the same street where another attack left eight dead just eight months earlier.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A desk job might be the reason you have bad sleep
item.description: Sedentary work habits and nonstandard schedules significantly increase the risk of sleep problems, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans Police can begin the process of ending federal oversight
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Police Department can begin ending its longstanding federal oversight, a judge ruled Tuesday in response to a request from the city and the Justice Department to wind down monitoring.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Octavia Butler imagined LA ravaged by fires. Her Altadena cemetery survived
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — Decades ago, the writer Octavia Butler had imagined a Los Angeles ravaged by fires. The Altadena cemetery where the science fiction and Afrofuturism author is buried did catch fire last week but suffered “minimal damage,” according to a statement on the cemetery's web site.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and CHRIS PIZZELLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Incident commanders say field damage inspections are ongoing and may change as new information is gathered and verified.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Michelle Obama's absence was notable given her tough past criticism of Trump, who succeeded her husband in office.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Blinken makes the case for post-war reconstruction, security and governance of Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken made a last-minute case Tuesday for a plan for the post-war reconstruction and governance of Gaza as a ceasefire between Israel and Hamas appears tantalizingly close to completion.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Blue city judge slammed for releasing violent suspect arrested again for vicious attack on teen
item.description: Seattle Judge Veronica Galvan released an armed robbery suspect who then allegedly viciously attacked a 14-year-old in what critics say is a soft-on-crime issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires latest: Firefighters prepare for dry conditions and strong winds
item.description: Firefighters around Los Angeles were preparing on Tuesday to attack flare-ups or new blazes. The National Weather Service issued a rare warning that dry winds combined with severely dry conditions created a “ particularly dangerous situation," indicating that any new fire could explode in size.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Obama will not attend Trump's inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Poll reveals how much voters blame climate change, Newsom for California fire crisis
item.description: In the wake of several deadly California wildfires a new poll shows how Americans are reacting to the disaster, which left at least two dozen people dead and reduced entire neighborhoods to charred rubble.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Sheriff calls on wildfire evacuees to be patient amid 'grim' search for bodies
item.description: Los Angeles County Sheriff Robert Luna asked community members to be patient with local officials, as residents eagerly await permission to return to their homes in southern California, where tens of thousands of acres have been decimated by wildfires in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Victims react to new intelligence community report that Havana Syndrome could be caused by a foreign actor
item.description: Some intelligence agencies are now suggesting a foreign adversary may be behind the mysterious "Havana Syndrome" injuries reportedly sustained by U.S. officials overseas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he's leaving Trump with a 'strong hand to play' in world conflicts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that his stewardship of American foreign policy has left the U.S. safer and economically more secure, arguing that President-elect Donald Trump will inherit a nation viewed as stronger and more reliable than it was four years ago.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, COLLEEN LONG and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Historic Powerball winner reportedly loses Malibu home in Palisades Fire
item.description: After winning $2.04 billion on a Powerball ticket in 2022, Edwin Castro reportedly bought multiple Los Angeles-area homes.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Heidi Montag tops iTunes chart after losing house in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Reality television star Heidi Montag's 2010 album “Superficial” has hit No. 1 on iTunes' all-genre songs and albums charts — 15 years after its release and just after Montag and husband Spencer Pratt lost their home in the Palisades Fire.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: How to save TikTok videos before the potential ban goes into effect
item.description: The deadline for a potential TikTok ban in the U.S. is fast approaching and many of the app's users are already taking steps to prepare, even as its fate remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Up to 4 in 10 people could develop dementia after 55. What you can do to lower your risk
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About a million Americans a year are expected to develop dementia by 2060, roughly double today’s toll, researchers reported Monday.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Navy veteran cleared of conspiracy charges in high-profile Jan. 6 case is sentenced to time served
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired Navy intelligence officer who was cleared of the most serious charges in his U.S. Capitol attack trial alongside Oath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes avoided additional time behind bars when he was sentenced on Friday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4
